verses.text: Manasseh reigns fifty-five years and does evil, rebuilding the high places his father had thrown down and rearing altars for Baalim. He undoes his father's reform. A wicked son can demolish in a few years the godly work of a faithful life.

Manasseh reigns fifty-five years and does evil, rebuilding the high places his father had thrown down and rearing altars for Baalim. He undoes his father's reform. A wicked son can demolish in a few years the godly work of a faithful life.

Manasseh reigns fifty-five years and does evil, rebuilding the high places his father had thrown down and rearing altars for Baalim. He undoes his father's reform. A wicked son can demolish in a few years the godly work of a faithful life.

He builds pagan altars even in the LORD's house, passes his children through the fire, and dabbles in witchcraft and familiar spirits, provoking God to anger. He sinks into the darkest of sins. A heart that rejects God's light gropes after every deceiving spirit.

He builds pagan altars even in the LORD's house, passes his children through the fire, and dabbles in witchcraft and familiar spirits, provoking God to anger. He sinks into the darkest of sins. A heart that rejects God's light gropes after every deceiving spirit.

He builds pagan altars even in the LORD's house, passes his children through the fire, and dabbles in witchcraft and familiar spirits, provoking God to anger. He sinks into the darkest of sins. A heart that rejects God's light gropes after every deceiving spirit.

Manasseh sets a carved idol in God's house and seduces Judah to do worse than the heathen the LORD destroyed. He leads a whole nation into ruin. The sin of a ruler is doubly grievous, for it drags the innocent down with him.

Manasseh sets a carved idol in God's house and seduces Judah to do worse than the heathen the LORD destroyed. He leads a whole nation into ruin. The sin of a ruler is doubly grievous, for it drags the innocent down with him.

Manasseh sets a carved idol in God's house and seduces Judah to do worse than the heathen the LORD destroyed. He leads a whole nation into ruin. The sin of a ruler is doubly grievous, for it drags the innocent down with him.

The LORD warns Manasseh and his people, but they will not listen, so He brings the Assyrian captains who take Manasseh with hooks and fetters to Babylon. Refused warnings are followed by chains. When mercy's word is despised, affliction says what words could not.

The LORD warns Manasseh and his people, but they will not listen, so He brings the Assyrian captains who take Manasseh with hooks and fetters to Babylon. Refused warnings are followed by chains. When mercy's word is despised, affliction says what words could not.

In his affliction Manasseh humbles himself greatly and prays, and God is entreated, brings him home, and restores him; 'then Manasseh knew that the LORD he was God.' No sinner is beyond mercy who truly turns. The chains that broke his pride opened his eyes to God.

In his affliction Manasseh humbles himself greatly and prays, and God is entreated, brings him home, and restores him; 'then Manasseh knew that the LORD he was God.' No sinner is beyond mercy who truly turns. The chains that broke his pride opened his eyes to God.

Restored, Manasseh rebuilds, removes the strange gods and idols, repairs the LORD's altar, and commands Judah to serve Him, though the people still sacrifice in the high places, but now to the LORD. Repentance bears fruit in reform. A changed heart works to undo the harm it once did.

Restored, Manasseh rebuilds, removes the strange gods and idols, repairs the LORD's altar, and commands Judah to serve Him, though the people still sacrifice in the high places, but now to the LORD. Repentance bears fruit in reform. A changed heart works to undo the harm it once did.

Restored, Manasseh rebuilds, removes the strange gods and idols, repairs the LORD's altar, and commands Judah to serve Him, though the people still sacrifice in the high places, but now to the LORD. Repentance bears fruit in reform. A changed heart works to undo the harm it once did.

The rest of his acts and his prayer to God are recorded; he dies and Amon his son reigns. A wicked life ends in grace, remembered for its prayer. God writes down the cry of the penitent, that later ages may know His mercy.

The rest of his acts and his prayer to God are recorded; he dies and Amon his son reigns. A wicked life ends in grace, remembered for its prayer. God writes down the cry of the penitent, that later ages may know His mercy.

The rest of his acts and his prayer to God are recorded; he dies and Amon his son reigns. A wicked life ends in grace, remembered for its prayer. God writes down the cry of the penitent, that later ages may know His mercy.

Amon does evil like his father once did, but 'humbled not himself... but trespassed more and more.' He copies the sin but not the repentance. To see another's pardon and still refuse to bow is to harden the heart past hope.

Amon does evil like his father once did, but 'humbled not himself... but trespassed more and more.' He copies the sin but not the repentance. To see another's pardon and still refuse to bow is to harden the heart past hope.

Amon does evil like his father once did, but 'humbled not himself... but trespassed more and more.' He copies the sin but not the repentance. To see another's pardon and still refuse to bow is to harden the heart past hope.

Amon's servants conspire and kill him in his house, but the people slay the conspirators and make Josiah king. Violence breeds violence, yet God preserves David's line. Out of a short and evil reign He brings forth the kingdom's greatest reformer.
